title: Database Access on DB2
text: Database Access on DB2 Logon Path In the assignment file under the [PATHS] section, enter a path in the following format: $DB2 DB2: { ServerName } : { Database } | { DB2UserName } | { DB2Password } or append it after a network path to access a DB2 database remotely: $DB2 Protocol : Machine + Port | OS-User | OS-Password + DB2: { ServerName }:{ Database } | { DB2UserName } | { DB2Password } ServerName —name of the database server local relational database directory entry (on iSeries) . Database —database name; optional if a default database is specified in the ODBC DSN (Data Source Name). DB2UserName and DB2Password —user and password for the DB2 database. The user name is not case-sensitive, but the password is. Optional for iSeries . For more information, see Logon Path on iSeries . Open and Close Support Both the open and close ProcScript commands are fully supported. Ownership Entities are created by default in a schema that is named after its creator (except on iSeries) . If you want to access entities owned by someone else, it is probable that the tables reside in a schema named after that person, and not in your default schema. To access them, you must direct these entities in the [ENTITIES] section of the assignment file. In the following example, all the entities in the application model ‘FRED’ are defined as belonging to ‘JANE’: [ENTITIES] *.FRED $DB2:JANE.*.* Copy The following assignments make the tables created and owned by user USYSDB2 available to other users: *.DICT $DB2:USYSDB2.*.* Copy You may want to tailor these assignments to control exactly which tables or entities users share and for which tables separate copies should be made. Related Links Database Access Uniface automatically opens the DBMS when a read or write ProcScript statement is issued. The path and logon details for the specific database (or databases) can be provided in an assignment file, a command line instruction, or in the open ProcScript instruction. Paths and Path Assignments A path is a named route to a resource required by the application, such as a database connector and database, a remote server, or a file location. open Open the specified DBMS or path, or connect to the Uniface Router. close Log off from the specified path or from all paths.
